name: Kindergarten Learning Commons — Semester 1
description: An 18-week, play-based Kindergarten pathway for questions, learning tools, collaboration, and digital care. Lessons are brief, hands-on, flexible, and designed for adult-guided learning.
learningGoals: Build confidence and curiosity with questions, learning tools, collaboration, and digital care.
Communicate learning through talk, drawing, movement, play, or a simple creation.
Practice safe, kind, and responsible participation.

lessons.title: Notice and talk: questions, learning tools, collaboration, and digital care
lessons.objective: I can notice details and share a question about questions, learning tools, collaboration, and digital care through a close look at questions, learning tools, collaboration, and digital care.

lessons.objective: I can notice details and share a question about questions, learning tools, collaboration, and digital care through a guided practice with questions, learning tools, collaboration, and digital care.

lessons.objective: I can notice details and share a question about questions, learning tools, collaboration, and digital care through a small creation or demonstration about questions, learning tools, collaboration, and digital care.

units.title: Wonder and notice: questions, learning tools, collaboration, and digital care
units.essentialQuestion: What can we notice and wonder about questions, learning tools, collaboration, and digital care?
outcomes: I can notice details and share a question about questions, learning tools, collaboration, and digital care.
I can use words, pictures, movement, or materials to communicate my thinking.

activities.title: A close look at questions, learning tools, collaboration, and digital care
activities.learnerAction: With an adult or teacher nearby when needed, the learner participates in a close look at questions, learning tools, collaboration, and digital care, uses words, pictures, movement, or materials to communicate thinking, and cleans up safely.

successCriteria: The learner participates safely and respectfully in questions, learning tools, collaboration, and digital care.
The learner communicates one observation, choice, or question using speech, drawing, movement, or a supported response.
